CREATE TABLE test_table_a (id INT, name STRING);
CREATE TABLE test_table_b (id INT, value INT);
COPY SELECT id, name FROM test_table_a INTO STDOUT;
COPY SELECT id, value FROM test_table_b USING DELIMITERS '|', NULL AS 'NULL' INTO STDOUT;
COPY SELECT a.id, a.name, b.value FROM test_table_a AS a JOIN test_table_b AS b ON a.id = b.id INTO STDOUT;
COPY SELECT id, name FROM test_table_a WHERE id IN (SELECT id FROM test_table_b) INTO STDOUT;
COPY SELECT id, name FROM test_table_a WHERE name LIKE 'A%' USING DELIMITERS '\t' INTO STDOUT;
COPY SELECT a.id, a.name FROM (SELECT * FROM test_table_a WHERE id > 1) AS a INTO STDOUT;
COPY SELECT id, name || ' - ' || CAST(id AS STRING) AS details FROM test_table_a INTO STDOUT USING DELIMITERS '|', record_separator '\n';
COPY SELECT a.id, b.value FROM test_table_a a LEFT JOIN test_table_b b ON a.id = b.id INTO STDOUT USING DELIMITERS '|', string_quote '"';
COPY (SELECT id, name FROM test_table_a WHERE id IN (1, 2) UNION SELECT id, value AS name FROM test_table_b) INTO STDOUT;
COPY SELECT DISTINCT a.name FROM test_table_a a JOIN test_table_b b ON a.id = b.id WHERE b.value > 150 INTO STDOUT;
COPY SELECT a.id, COUNT(b.id) FROM test_table_a a LEFT JOIN test_table_b b ON a.id = b.id GROUP BY a.id INTO STDOUT;
